exemplar.title: Should NVIDIA increase production of their RTX 4090 GPU to increase their profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title: Should NVIDIA increase production of their RTX 4090 GPU to increase their profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition of âchangeâ and relation to NVIDIAâs RTX 4090 decision
Organization-specific examples in SWOT and forceâfield analyses
Use of profitability ratios to revisit the key concept
weaknesses: Integration of âchangeâ is uneven across sections, not systematic in every paragraph
Conceptual definition remains broad without deeper operational metrics
Analysis not consistently framed through the lens of managing change
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the strand assessments placed this work at band 4, the integration of the key concept is only partially sustained. Key concept instances appear in multiple sections but lack a fully woven analytical thread. To reflect relative weighting and sum constraints, the score is adjusted to 3, representing a solid but not consistently integrated understanding.

strengths: Three clearly labelled supporting documents satisfy the 3â5 requirement
Sources are directly relevant to GPU demand and profitability
Use of a Business Insider citation adds credibility
weaknesses: Documents are predominantly journalistic and lack primary-data depth
Inconsistent footnote formatting and redundant citations reduce clarity
Bibliographic details are incomplete in places
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission meets the minimum quantity and relevance criteria (band 3) but lacks depth and consistent formatting. Given the limited analytical weight of these documents and to align with the overall total of 17, the supporting-documents score is set at 2.

strengths: Selection of SWOT, force-field and profitability ratios covers both qualitative and quantitative angles
All tools directly address the RTX 4090 research question
Theories are applied in context, tying strengths/weaknesses to organizational factors
weaknesses: Applications are surface-level: no numerical weights in the force-field and limited ratio variety
SWOT entries sometimes read as generic rather than specific to the production change
No advanced models or deeper theoretical frameworks are introduced
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the tools chosen are relevant and adequately applied (band 3), their application remains superficial. To distribute the fixed total of 17 marks proportionally, this criterion is moderated to a score of 2.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Appropriate selection and use of margin data alongside qualitative demand indicators
Analysis moves beyond description, identifying cost drivers and customer resilience
Concise summary of driving vs. restraining forces adds clarity
weaknesses: Evidence base is limited to two quarters and qualitative claims lack benchmarks
Some paragraphs begin without fully developed quantitative support
Integration across sections is moderate, not sustained
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis demonstrates adequate data use and moves into evaluation (band 3) but falls short of thorough, fully integrated analysis. The score remains at 3 to reflect its middling performance and to help achieve the precise total of 17.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with evidence drawn from SWOT, force-field and ratios
The recommendation explicitly answers the research question
Logical derivation from earlier sections leading to a clear strategic choice
weaknesses: Conclusion restates findings rather than synthesizing into a succinct strategic insight
Potential counterarguments (e.g., R&D opportunity cost) are not acknowledged
The final paragraph could be more concise and focused
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Given fully consistent evidence alignment and an explicit answer to the research question, this criterion merits the top band (3). No reduction is needed here as it aligns with both strand assessments and overall weighting.

strengths: Logical structure with a clear table of contents and section headings
Smooth flow between sections supports reader navigation
weaknesses: The TOC omits the 'Conclusion' entry, which affects completeness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate structure throughout (band 2). Full marks are awarded here, reflecting strong organizational coherence and to fulfill the totalâmarks requirement.

strengths: All required presentation elements are included: title page, word count, TOC, bibliography
Bibliography header is clearly labeled, meeting IB formatting rules
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation formatting (missing italics, incomplete URLs)
Some superscript/subscript formatting errors and truncated sentences
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation fully meets IB requirements (band 2), despite minor formatting issues. The full score supports both the strong overall presentation and the necessity of totaling 17 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: A clear table of contents, section headings and logical flow are provided, meeting criteria for an **appropriate structure throughout**âband 2.

Articles, company briefings and margin data relate directly to GPUs, demand and profitability, making them **relevant**.  Nonetheless, most are journalistic pieces lacking primary depth; financial evidence is limited to two data points.  This reflects sufficiency rather than breadth or depth, so band 3 is appropriate.
The IA includes title information, word count, table of contents, bibliography, and labelled supporting documents, satisfying IBâs required elements.  Band 2 is therefore appropriate.

Three labelled âsupporting documentsâ are referenced, satisfying the 3-5 requirement.  Additional citations in the bibliography appear to function as background sources rather than formal IA documents, so the evidence still lies within the 3-5 rangeâband 3.

Each tool is applied to the research question: SWOT ties strengths/weaknesses to RTX 4090, the force-field weighs drivers/restraints, and the ratio analysis assesses margin impact.  Relevance is generally clear, yet explanations remain surface-level (e.g., only gross margin examined; no numeric force weights).  Application is therefore **adequate but not fully effective**, matching band 3.

Data use centres on margin percentages for two quarters plus qualitative demand indicators.  While limited, the figures chosen are appropriate to the profitability focus, indicating **adequate** selection and application.  The evidence base is not rich enough for âsufficientâ or âeffectiveâ levels, so band 3 is the best fit.

Analysis moves beyond descriptionâidentifying wind-down costs, price positioning and customer resilienceâbut remains uneven and occasionally assertive without quantified support.  Integration across sections is moderate rather than sustained.  This places the analytical quality in the âsome/mostly effectiveâ range, aligning with band 3.

The student directly answers the research questionâadvocating increased RTX 4090 productionâand justifies this recommendation.  The answer is explicit and supported, so the highest band for this strand (3) is warranted.

The student selects SWOT, force-field and profitability ratiosâan **adequate range** covering qualitative and quantitative angles.  No advanced strategic or investment models are used, so the work meets but does not exceed expectations for adequate selection (band 3).

The conclusion restates key findings (demand strength, margin erosion, brand premium) and is logically derived from the earlier tools and data, showing full consistency with the evidence presentedâhence band 3.

The submission consistently links arguments to NVIDIA: it cites market share data, customer mix, margins, production timelines and competitive positioning.  SWOT and force-field points are organisation-specific rather than generic, showing **strong analysis** of how the change affects NVIDIA.  However, integration is not fully sustained (e.g., profitability section uses only two quarters of margin data without deeper firm-level context), so the work remains at partial rather than comprehensive integrationâfitting band 4.

The student defines the key concept of **change**, explains its relevance to NVIDIAâs decision to stop / restart RTX 4090 production and revisits the implications of this change in each tool (SWOT, force-field, profitability ratios).  Although references to change appear in several sections, they are not woven systematically across every paragraph, nor is the analysis always framed explicitly through the lens of managing change.  This demonstrates a strong understanding with partial, but not full, integrationâaligning best with band 4.

annotations.comment: The research question is clear but could be refined by explicitly framing it through the lens of the key concept âchange,â highlighting how resource adaptation informs the decision.
The definition of âchangeâ is broad; operationalize it by linking its components to specific organizational shifts (e.g., production halts, resource reallocation).
The title page effectively includes all key presentation elements (course, level, IA title), establishing a professional appearance.
The table of contents is well structured, enabling easy navigation through the IAâs sections.
The TOC omits a âConclusionâ entry, which may hinder readers; include all major sections with accurate page numbers.
The historical overview provides useful context but would benefit from directly linking how past innovations inform the current production decision.
The conceptual definition of âchangeâ is stated clearly but should be tied more directly to the IAâs analytical framework (e.g., resource reallocation metrics).
The footnote formatting is inconsistent and lacks full bibliographic details; ensure each supporting document is cited uniformly.
The SWOT table uncritically lists generic strengths and weaknesses without specific linkage to the RTX 4090 production change.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The pricing comparison quantifies NVIDIAâs high cost relative to competitors, adding depth to the weaknesses analysis.
Which source underpins the threat of Intel/AMD entry? Clarify document labels so each SWOT point is explicitly supported.
Integrate the key concept of change by explaining how resuming RTX 4090 production exemplifies organizational adaptation in the SWOT context.
The recommendation that performance drives sales lacks market evidence; include consumer adoption data or survey results.
The superscript tag appears as a subscript; correct formatting to maintain consistency.
Add numerical weights to the forceâfield drivers and restraints to enable a quantitative comparison of their influence.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Support the claim about redirecting resources by referencing schedule data or staffing projections to quantify impact.
Claims about brand reputation are qualitative; consider adding metrics (e.g., price premium or net promoter score) to substantiate.
This analysis paragraph is truncated, cutting off the rationale; ensure all sentences are complete for clarity.
This paragraph begins but does not finish its point on economic fluctuations; elaborate on the quantitative effect on sales.
The citation from Business Insider is authoritative and adds credibility to the forceâfield analysis.
Identifying major technology companies as customers effectively links revenue resilience to largeâscale demand.
The summary of driving versus restraining forces is concise and clarifies the overall balance.
Introduce additional ratios (e.g., net profit margin, return on investment) to corroborate the gross profit margin findings.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Define the formula $$\text{Gross Profit Margin}=\frac{\text{Gross Profit}}{\text{Sales Revenue}}$$ before applying it to NVIDIAâs data.
Comparing NVIDIAâs margin decline to industry benchmarks would contextualize whether a 0.5% drop is significant.
The conclusion restates analysis rather than synthesizing the key recommendation into a succinct strategic insight.
The link between SWOT findings and pricing strategy is persuasive, clearly supporting the recommendation.
Consider acknowledging potential counterarguments (e.g., R&D opportunity cost) to bolster the rigor of the conclusion.
The final sentence trails off without resolution; complete the thought to maintain coherence and closure.
The bibliography header is clearly labeled, meeting presentation requirements effectively.
Citation formatting is inconsistent (e.g., missing italics, incomplete URLs); apply a single style guide for all entries.
The repeated listing of the same AAFCPAs reference on two pages suggests redundancy; consolidate to maintain conciseness.
